result:
**Goal: Add an Earthquake & Seismic Wave Propagation mode.**

A Burridge-Knopoff spring-block fault model where tectonic stress accumulates on a heterogeneous fault plane until stick-slip ruptures cascade as earthquakes, radiating P-waves and S-waves through layered crust. The model naturally produces emergent power-law statistics (Gutenberg-Richter frequency-magnitude scaling), aftershock clustering (Omori's law), and characteristic earthquake cycles — all from simple local rules. Presets: **Strike-Slip Fault** (San Andreas-style lateral rupture), **Subduction Zone** (megathrust with deep coupling), **Swarm Seismicity** (diffuse volcanic/geothermal cluster), **Tsunami Generation** (seafloor displacement driving shallow-water wave), **Induced Seismicity** (injection-triggered fault reactivation), and **Coulomb Stress Transfer** (one earthquake triggering the next via static stress changes).

This fills a gap between the existing Tectonic Plates mode (large-scale plate motion) and Wave Equation mode (generic waves) by focusing on the fault-scale rupture dynamics and seismic radiation that neither covers — and it's a showcase for self-organized criticality, one of the deepest ideas in complexity science.

Shall I build it?
